6:["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"pt-6 pb-2","children":["$","nav",null,{"aria-label":"Breadcrumb","children":["$","ol",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","li","0",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[false,["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/","className":"hover:text-orange-500 transition-colors","children":"Home"}]]}],["$","li","1",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"m9 18 6-6-6-6","key":"mthhwq"}]],"className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog","className":"hover:text-orange-500 transition-colors","children":"Blog"}]]}],["$","li","2",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":"$6:props:children:0:props:children:props:children:props:children:1:props:children:0:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Productivity","className":"hover:text-orange-500 transition-colors","children":"Productivity"}]]}],["$","li","3",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":"$6:props:children:0:props:children:props:children:props:children:1:props:children:0:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","span",null,{"className":"text-stone-600 font-medium line-clamp-1","children":"Pomodoro Technique: The Complete Guide to Working in Focused Sprints"}]]}]]}]}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 sm:p-8 mt-4 shadow-lg border border-stone-100 dark:border-stone-800 mb-10","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-bold text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 leading-tight mb-5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Pomodoro Technique: The Complete Guide to Working in Focused Sprints"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/sara-osei","className":"flex items-center gap-2 group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1438761681033-6461ffad8d80?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Sara Osei","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"Sara Osei"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Productivity Researcher"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","9 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"January 17, 2026"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"The Pomodoro Technique is one of the most researched and widely used productivity methods — for good reason. This guide explains the system, why it works, how to adapt it, and the mistakes that make it fail."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"what-the-pomodoro-technique-actually-is","children":"What the Pomodoro Technique Actually Is"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The Pomodoro Technique was developed by Francesco Cirillo in the late 1980s. It is structurally simple:"}}],["$","ol","2",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Choose a single task to work on"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Set a timer for 25 minutes"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Work on only that task until the timer rings"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Take a 5-minute break"}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Repeat. After four cycles, take a longer break (15–30 minutes)"}}]]}],"$L9","$La","$Lb","$Lc","$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a","$L1b","$L1c","$L1d","$L1e","$L1f","$L20","$L21","$L22","$L23","$L24","$L25","$L26"]}],"$L27","$L28","$L29"]}],"$L2a"]}]]}]
7:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
8:["$","$L2b",null,{"children":["$","$2c",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@2d"}]}]
9:["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The name comes from the tomato-shaped kitchen timer Cirillo used as a student (pomodoro is Italian for tomato). The technique is now one of the most studied and widely adopted productivity methods in the world."}}]
a:["$","h2","4",{"id":"why-it-works","children":"Why It Works"}]
b:["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The Pomodoro Technique is effective for several distinct reasons."}}]
c:["$","h3","6",{"id":"it-makes-time-visible","children":"It Makes Time Visible"}]
d:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Most people have a poor intuitive sense of how long tasks take. The 25-minute timer transforms abstract working time into a concrete, bounded unit. You can feel 25 minutes. You cannot feel \"a productive afternoon.\""}}]
e:["$","h3","8",{"id":"it-creates-commitment","children":"It Creates Commitment"}]
f:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Choosing one task before starting the timer creates a micro-commitment. Research on implementation intentions shows that specifying when, where, and what you will do significantly increases the likelihood of doing it."}}]
10:["$","h3","10",{"id":"it-legalises-breaks","children":"It Legalises Breaks"}]
11:["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Many people feel guilty about taking breaks. The structured break after each pomodoro reframes rest as part of the system rather than a deviation from it. Paradoxically, scheduling breaks reduces the urge to take unscheduled ones."}}]
12:["$","h3","12",{"id":"it-makes-interruptions-visible","children":"It Makes Interruptions Visible"}]
13:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"During a pomodoro, you agree not to engage with anything not related to the current task. If something interrupts you — a thought, a notification, a colleague — you note it on a \"later\" list and return to it after the pomodoro ends. Over time, you develop a realistic picture of how often you are interrupted."}}]
14:["$","h2","14",{"id":"how-to-implement-it","children":"How to Implement It"}]
15:["$","h3","15",{"id":"the-classic-version","children":"The Classic Version"}]
16:["$","ul","16",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Set timer: 25 minutes"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Work: uninterrupted focus on one task"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Break: 5 minutes (stand up, move, no screens)"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"After four pomodoros: 20–30 minute break"}}]]}]
17:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Use a physical timer, not your phone. Your phone is an interruption machine. A dedicated timer keeps the phone out of reach."}}]
18:["$","h3","18",{"id":"the-interruption-protocol","children":"The Interruption Protocol"}]
19:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"When something interrupts you during a pomodoro:"}}]
1a:["$","ul","20",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Internal interruption</strong> (you think of something else): Write it on a list and return immediately to the current task"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>External interruption</strong> (a colleague, a notification): Note it, ask them to wait, return to the task. If it is genuinely urgent: end the pomodoro, handle it, start fresh"}}]]}]
1b:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A pomodoro that is interrupted does not count. This creates a healthy incentive to protect the 25-minute window."}}]
1c:["$","h2","22",{"id":"adapting-the-technique","children":"Adapting the Technique"}]
1d:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The 25/5 split is a starting point, not a law."}}]
1e:["$","p","24",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Longer pomodoros (45–50 minutes)</strong>: Better for tasks requiring extended warm-up time — writing, deep analysis, coding. The 25-minute default can feel like it ends just as you hit your stride."}}]
1f:["$","p","25",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Shorter pomodoros (15 minutes)</strong>: Better for tasks you are procrastinating on. A 15-minute commitment is psychologically easy to start. Once started, you are often engaged enough to continue."}}]
20:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Variable breaks</strong>: A 5-minute break after a 25-minute session is fine. After a difficult 45-minute session, 10–15 minutes is more restorative."}}]
21:["$","p","27",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The right variant is the one you actually use. Experiment for two weeks before concluding the technique does not work for you."}}]
22:["$","h2","28",{"id":"when-the-pomodoro-technique-does-not-work","children":"When the Pomodoro Technique Does Not Work"}]
23:["$","p","29",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Creative or flow states</strong>: Interrupting deep creative work every 25 minutes can be counterproductive. If you regularly find yourself in genuine flow, extend your pomodoros to 60–90 minutes for creative sessions."}}]
24:["$","p","30",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Highly collaborative roles</strong>: If your work requires constant availability to colleagues, the strict no-interruptions requirement conflicts with job demands. Use pomodoros for independent work blocks."}}]
25:["$","p","31",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Tasks with natural completion points</strong>: Some tasks do not benefit from arbitrary time limits. For these, complete the natural unit of work rather than stopping at the timer."}}]
26:["$","p","32",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For structuring your full day around focused blocks, see our guide on <a href=\"/blog/time-blocking-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">time blocking</a>."}}]
27:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400","children":"Topic:"}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Productivity","className":"text-sm font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-green-50 text-green-700 hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity","children":"Productivity"}],[["$","$L3","pomodoro",{"href":"/blog/tag/pomodoro","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 transition-colors","children":["#","pomodoro"]}],["$","$L3","focus",{"href":"/blog/tag/focus","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","focus"]}],["$","$L3","time-management",{"href":"/blog/tag/time-management","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","time-management"]}],["$","$L3","productivity",{"href":"/blog/tag/productivity","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","productivity"]}],["$","$L3","techniques",{"href":"/blog/tag/techniques","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","techniques"]}]]]}]
28:["$","$L2e",null,{"title":"Pomodoro Technique: The Complete Guide to Working in Focused Sprints","slug":"pomodoro-technique-guide"}]
29:["$","div",null,{"className":"print:hidden","children":["$","$L2f",null,{}]}]
2a:["$","aside",null,{"className":"space-y-5 print:hidden","children":[["$","$L30",null,{"items":[{"id":"what-the-pomodoro-technique-actually-is","text":"What the Pomodoro Technique Actually Is","level":2},{"id":"why-it-works","text":"Why It Works","level":2},{"id":"it-makes-time-visible","text":"It Makes Time Visible","level":3},{"id":"it-creates-commitment","text":"It Creates Commitment","level":3},{"id":"it-legalises-breaks","text":"It Legalises Breaks","level":3},{"id":"it-makes-interruptions-visible","text":"It Makes Interruptions Visible","level":3},{"id":"how-to-implement-it","text":"How to Implement It","level":2},{"id":"the-classic-version","text":"The Classic Version","level":3},{"id":"the-interruption-protocol","text":"The Interruption Protocol","level":3},{"id":"adapting-the-technique","text":"Adapting the Technique","level":2},{"id":"when-the-pomodoro-technique-does-not-work","text":"When the Pomodoro Technique Does Not Work","level":2}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1438761681033-6461ffad8d80?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Sara Osei","className":"w-14 h-14 rounded-2xl object-cover object-top mb-4"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"font-bold text-lg text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 mb-0.5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Sara Osei"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-orange-500 font-medium mb-3","children":"Productivity Researcher"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Studies how physical and digital environments affect focus and output. Her writing bridges neuroscience and practical advice."}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/sara-osei","className":"text-xs font-semibold text-orange-500 hover:text-orange-600
